text:
Once upon a time, in a verdant forest clearing, a squirrel gathered acorns. Every day it would fill its basket, dreaming of winter. It mused on its life.

One evening, a wise owl perched nearby. "What are you thinking about?" it asked with a knowing glance.

The squirrel paused. "Just about that winter," it replied contemplatively. "You think?"

The owl tilted its head. "Well," it said thoughtfully, "every seed-bearing branch dreams of fall." It leaned closer. "You spend your days dreaming of warm spots to sleep... someday you'll find them."

The squirrel blinked. "Seeds?" It smiled.

"Yes," the owl continued. "Each acorn you gather represents hope. You believe it'll grow into a big oak tree, protecting you and your fellow squirrels."

The squirrel pondered this profound wisdom. A chill ran down its spine. Perhaps this forest was more than just about acorns? Maybe it was about
